welcome.title: Welcome to the Den of the Fallen
paragraphs: This small site came to life because someone wanted a place to publish their little stories. So they searched the Internet for somewhere to share them.

But between platforms where everything is imposed, reshaped into social networks, and everything that comes with them, I ended up understanding one simple thing: we had forgotten what a story is meant to do.
An author is human, carrying an idea. Whether you like that idea or not, they do everything they can to bring it to life. Yet too many people write for the audience instead of writing for themselves.
Here, I want to build a haven for authors who choose the story they imagine first, authors who breathe life into words so readers can feel them.
I cannot promise that every reader will find exactly what they are looking for, but I hope that discovering stories can become a pleasure again.
A story is a thousand ways to escape, to imagine, to cry.
The only limit is your imagination.
Authors, return to your forge and make your ideas felt.
Readers, simply enjoy what you discover.
But never forget: respect is never optional, whether inside the story or in your exchanges.

conditionsOfUse.title: Terms of Use
conditionsOfUse.html:
Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo

Terms of Use

Dedicated reading page. The summary below lets you jump directly to each section without leaving the site.

Summary

1. Purpose of the Terms of Use

These Terms of Use define the rules for accessing, browsing and using Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo, as well as the rights and obligations of its users.

The site allows users to read stories, create an account, publish and manage works and chapters, comment, follow stories, and access certain features reserved for registered members.

Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo is currently available free of charge. However, if the site’s growth and operating costs require it, a donation system may later be introduced to help maintain it. The site is not intended to be funded through advertising.

2. Site access and account creation

Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo can be accessed publicly for reading, without creating an account. Any visitor may browse stories and read chapters freely, provided these Terms of Use are respected.

Access to the site’s other features is reserved for users with an account. Creating an account is required in particular to comment, follow stories, access personal features, publish works, manage chapters and use member-only spaces.

Each user is solely responsible for their account, credentials and password confidentiality. It is their responsibility not to share access with a third party and to take every useful measure to preserve account security. Any action performed through an account is considered to have been performed under that account holder’s responsibility.

The site is open to everyone. However, published works may carry age ratings and content warnings. Authors are responsible for correctly rating their works according to the site’s rules. Minors remain under the responsibility of their legal guardians. Neither the site nor the authors may be held responsible if a minor deliberately bypasses warnings, ratings or reading restrictions to access content not intended for them.

Any use of the site that violates these Terms of Use, the site rules or applicable law may lead to content removal, account suspension or account deletion, without prior notice depending on the seriousness of the situation. Any hateful, racist, sexist, discriminatory content or conduct, any glorification of violence, or any incitement to illegal acts is strictly forbidden and may result in an immediate ban without prior warning.

3. Publishing rules

Each author is fully responsible for the content they publish on the site, whether it is a work, a chapter, a synopsis, a cover image or any other element linked to their publication.

Every published work must respect the site rules, these Terms of Use and applicable law. It is forbidden to publish illegal, defamatory, fraudulent, misleading, impersonating or rights-infringing content.

An author may publish only content for which they actually hold the necessary rights.

Fanfictions are allowed. However, when a work is based on a universe, story or elements from an existing work, this must be clearly stated at the end of the synopsis. The author must explicitly mention the original work, for example using a formula such as:

“Work created on the basis of story X.”

The site does not impose a separate tag between original works and fanfiction: each author is therefore responsible for properly crediting the base they use.

Translations, adaptations or reposts of a work that does not belong to the person publishing it are forbidden without clear authorization from the original author. In case of doubt or report, the person who published the content may be contacted so they can provide proof of authorization or quickly remove the work concerned. If necessary, the original author may also be warned that their story is being used, especially in the case of publication in another language without established permission.

When an author publishes a work they created themselves, whether it is an original work or fanfiction they wrote themselves, they keep all rights over their text, interpretation, structure and own content. The site is not intended to appropriate those rights, and no unauthorized copy, reuse or exploitation is allowed.

Cover images must also respect applicable rights. An image found online may be used only if its use is authorized, and it must be credited when necessary. Similarly, when an image comes from an artist, that artist must be credited if the conditions of use of the work require it. AI-generated images are allowed, provided they do not obviously reproduce or directly repurpose images from existing works in an explicit way.

Even within fiction, no author may exceed the limits set by law, the site rules or the rights of others.

4. Work ratings and sensitive content

In order to protect readers, make works easier to understand by age category, and maintain a clear editorial line, each author must choose the rating of their story seriously, as well as whether the sensitive-content marker should be enabled.

The rating system is not there to censor a work or judge its quality. It exists to clearly tell readers how harsh the content is and how certain subjects are handled.

The sensitive-content marker complements the age rating. It warns that a work contains themes or situations that may affect some readers, even when those elements remain compatible with the chosen age range.

The author is responsible for correctly rating their work.

Quick access to section 4

4.1. General audience

The General audience rating is reserved for works accessible to all readers.

The tone may be serious, dark or emotionally strong, but the content must remain readable without exposing the reader to sensitive, explicit or shocking elements. Difficult themes must be absent, or only mentioned very lightly, without harsh impact, ambiguous scenes or disturbing detail.

In this category, sensitive content is strictly forbidden.

This means, in particular, that the work must not contain:

  • harsh violence
  • sexual violence
  • rape or attempted rape, even when heavily implied
  • shocking bodily descriptions
  • high-impact psychological scenes linked to abuse, torture, mistreatment or extreme humiliation

A work rated General audience must be readable without requiring a specific warning.

4.2. R15

The R15 rating allows more serious, harsher or emotionally heavier works, while remaining suitable for a warned younger audience.

At this level, some sensitive subjects may exist, but they must be handled with restraint. The work may refer to serious events, violence, trauma or difficult realities, but it must not dwell on them in a frontal way.

When a serious theme is handled in R15, it must remain:

  • brief
  • lightly detailed
  • narratively framed
  • non-graphic
  • non-voyeuristic

For example, a grave act may be mentioned as having happened, but without describing how it unfolded, without emphasized staging, and without seeking a raw impact. The reader may understand that an event occurred, without being exposed to its execution or material detail.

The sensitive-content marker may be used in R15 if the story addresses delicate themes, but always in a softened and indirect form.

4.3. R16

The R16 rating corresponds to works that are harsher, darker or heavier psychologically.

This category allows a deeper treatment of certain difficult themes. Without the sensitive-content marker, the work may already clearly suggest harsh realities, as long as it avoids showing or detailing the acts themselves.

With the sensitive-content marker, the author may go further into consequences, psychological tension, emotional weight and certain narrative details, provided one clear limit is respected: the acts themselves must not be shown or described explicitly.

In R16, it is possible:

  • to emphasize the impact of an act
  • to show trauma, suffering, physical or mental repercussions
  • to explore the consequences on characters in depth
  • to make the subject clearly understandable

However, it remains forbidden:

  • to precisely describe the gesture or act
  • to show the act in detail
  • to seek shock value through direct description
  • to turn a sensitive scene into a graphic demonstration

In other words, in R16, the subject may be fully understood and its impact may be developed, but the act itself is not shown.

4.4. R18

The R18 rating is reserved for works intended for an adult audience.

This does not mean that everything is allowed. R18 is not a rule-free zone. It allows adult, serious or violent subjects to be addressed more freely, but always within a clear editorial framework.

Without the sensitive-content marker, an R18 work may already handle difficult themes in a direct, mature and assumed way. With the sensitive-content marker, the author may go further into harshness, the violence of consequences or the darkness of certain situations.

However, even in R18, certain limits remain in place.

The following are forbidden:

  • pornographic content
  • detailed descriptions of sexual acts
  • detailed descriptions of violent gestures or acts
  • complacency in the execution of an act
  • graphic staging of rape, torture or mutilation
  • writing centered on the act itself rather than on its meaning, impact or consequences

In R18, it is therefore possible:

  • to address an adult subject directly
  • to describe physical consequences more bluntly
  • to show the state of an injured body
  • to explain certain realities if it serves the story
  • to handle heavy scenes with greater intensity

But it remains forbidden to detail the act in its precise unfolding.

In summary:

R18 allows more harshness, more darkness and more directness,

but not pornography, not detailed descriptions of acts, not lingering over the act itself, and not complacency.

4.5. Role of the “sensitive content” marker

The sensitive-content marker is not an independent age rating. It is an additional warning.

It indicates that a work contains elements that may affect some readers, even if those elements remain compatible with the chosen age rating.

This marker must be enabled when a work addresses, even without explicit scenes:

  • heavy violence
  • abuse
  • trauma
  • assault
  • mistreatment
  • psychologically harsh situations
  • morally or emotionally difficult subjects

The sensitive-content marker therefore lets readers know that they are entering a more demanding work.

It does not give the right to exceed the limits of the chosen rating.

It does not replace correct rating of the work.

It does not automatically make content acceptable if it violates the rules above.

4.6. General principle to respect

Whatever the rating level, one rule remains constant:

the impact may be shown more than the act.

A work may be harsh, heavy, tragic or violent without needing to describe an act frontally in detail. What matters is the coherence of the treatment, control of tone, and respect for the limits of the chosen category.

The purpose of this rating system is to protect readers, help authors position their works clearly, and allow serious publication without falling into excess or vagueness.

4.7. Author responsibility

Each author is responsible for how they rate their work and for whether the sensitive-content marker is present.

If a work does not respect these rules, the author will be contacted by email at the address linked to the story concerned. The message will specify the requested changes.

From the sending of that email, the author will have 7 days to update their story.

If, after that period, the requested changes have not been made, the story may be removed in its entirety.

In that case, the author may also receive particular attention for future publications.

If three warnings are ignored or not respected, the author’s account may be deleted entirely.

By publishing on the site, the author acknowledges having read these rules and agrees to respect them.

5. Comments and user conduct

Comments must remain directly related to the story or chapter concerned. They are meant to react to the work, discuss its content or share reading feedback, not to divert the discussion space for other purposes.

The following behavior is forbidden in comments and, more broadly, on the site:

  • insulting, threatening, harassing or humiliating remarks
  • hateful, racist, sexist, discriminatory or illegal content
  • gratuitous provocation and deliberately harmful behavior
  • spam or abusive repetition of messages
  • advertising or unauthorized self-promotion

Comments are not advertising space. Each comment area exists for the story or chapter to which it is attached. It is not meant to promote another work, another author, another piece of content or an external activity, unless the author concerned explicitly allows it.

In case of error, omission or adjustment, users are invited to edit their comment rather than multiply unnecessary new messages.

A comment may be deleted at any time by:

  • its author
  • the author of the story concerned
  • site moderation

No claim may be made following the deletion of a comment. The author of a work remains free to moderate the discussion space linked to their own content.

Moderation also reserves the right to delete any problematic comment and take any appropriate measure depending on the seriousness of the situation.

At this time, no formal warning system is guaranteed. As a result, each user remains fully responsible for their behavior on the site. In case of abuse, repeated nuisance or behavior clearly contrary to these Terms of Use, moderation may intervene freely, including by deleting a comment or applying a stronger sanction if the situation justifies it.

Until an integrated reporting system is available, authors and readers may contact the site by email to report problematic behavior or comments, specifying the story, chapter and element concerned as precisely as possible to make the report easier to process.

6. Intellectual property

Each author keeps all rights over the works, chapters, texts, worlds and content they publish on the site, provided they are indeed the creator or have the necessary authorization to publish them.

Publishing a work on Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo grants no exclusivity. An author remains free to publish their work elsewhere if they wish. However, the site may not be used as a promotional medium toward another platform. Any advertising, promotional link or redirect intended to send readers away to another publishing site may lead to the immediate removal of the content concerned.

An author may delete their work from the site at any time. The site claims no exclusive rights and no ownership over a work removed by its author.

However, the name Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo, its visual identity, presentation, own graphic elements, own images, organization and all elements created specifically for the site are the property of its owner. These elements may not be copied, reused, reproduced or exploited without authorization.

Works published by authors, like site-specific elements, may not be copied, reproduced, republished, redistributed or reused without authorization. Any unauthorized reuse attempt, whether it concerns a work, a cover, visual content or a site-specific element, may lead to any measure deemed necessary.

The site may implement technical measures intended to limit unauthorized copying or retrieval of content. Whether such technical protection exists or not does not remove the prohibition against copying or reusing content without authorization.

7. Liability limits

Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo makes every effort to provide operation that is as stable, clear and accessible as possible. However, continuous operation without error, interruption or technical incident cannot be guaranteed.

The user acknowledges that use of the site is at their own responsibility. The site cannot be held responsible for a bug, temporary outage, unavailability, malfunction, display error, loss of access or loss of content linked to a technical incident.

Authors are expressly encouraged to keep their own backups. Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo is a publishing site, not a guaranteed storage or backup service.

The site’s operation relies on external technical services and infrastructure, particularly for hosting and data storage. As a result, the site cannot be held responsible for loss, alteration or unavailability of data resulting from a technical incident, third-party service failure or an event beyond its control.

8. Personal data

Use of the site may involve the processing of certain personal data necessary for its operation, including account creation, login, publishing stories, comments and other features reserved for registered members.

Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo does not sell users’ personal data.

Data linked to the site’s operation is hosted and processed by third-party technical services, including Supabase, which handles part of the storage, authentication and technical infrastructure of the site.

The site owner cannot guarantee or control the processing performed by those third-party providers outside the use necessary for site operation. For additional information about the conditions, commitments and rules specific to the infrastructure used, users are invited to consult Supabase documents directly at the following addresses:

  • https://supabase.com/aup
  • https://supabase.com/terms

For data use within Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo itself, users are invited to consult the site’s Privacy Policy.

9. User rights over their data and account

Each user has rights over the data linked to their account and use of the site, including access, correction and deletion.

When the site tools allow it, the user should primarily use their own settings to manage their account, information and content.

Account deletion may be performed directly by the user from the site settings. This deletion is immediate. Confirmation is requested before validation. Once deletion is confirmed, all data linked to the account is deleted, including but not limited to the profile, comments, stories, chapters, favorites and other content attached to the account concerned.

This deletion is final.

In case of a serious problem that cannot be solved directly by the user, including hacking or fraudulent account access, the site owner may be contacted, provided the request includes sufficiently serious elements or proof justifying the requested intervention.

If an account is deleted by moderation or site administration, some data or elements may be temporarily kept as evidence when such retention is necessary to handle a dispute, respond to a claim, establish facts or protect the people concerned. This retention may occur only within the limits of what is strictly necessary to handle the problem encountered.

For additional information about personal data and its processing, users are invited to consult the Privacy Policy.

10. Changes to the Terms of Use

These Terms of Use may be modified at any time to reflect the evolution of the site, its features, operating needs or applicable legal obligations.

The version in force is the one published on the site at the time of use.

In case of an important change, the site may inform users by any means considered appropriate.

Continuing to use the site after the Terms of Use are updated means accepting the version then in force.

11. Contact

For any question about the site, its rules, published content, a technical issue or a request that cannot be solved directly by the user, the site may be contacted:

by email at: nitroapex@gmail.com

through the site’s Discord server, accessible from the links present on the site

The site reserves the right to reply through whichever channel it considers most appropriate depending on the nature of the request.

legalNotice.title: Legal Notice
legalNotice.html:
Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo

Legal Notice

1. Site publisher

Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo is published on a non-professional basis.

For any question about the site, contact: nitroapex@gmail.com

2. Public hosting

The public hosting and display of the site are provided through GitHub Pages, a service operated by:

GitHub, Inc.,
88 Colin P. Kelly Jr. St,
San Francisco,
CA 94107,
United States.

3. Technical infrastructure for accounts and data

Supabase, Inc.

OCBC Centre,
Singapore 049513.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.html:
Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo

Privacy Policy

1. Purpose

This Privacy Policy explains how Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo uses the data necessary for the site to function.

2. Data concerned

The site may use data linked to user accounts and site features, including:

  • the email address linked to the account
  • profile information entered by the user
  • content published by the user
  • comments posted on the site
  • technical information required for login, authentication and account maintenance

3. Purpose of use

This data is used only to operate the site and its features, including:

  • creating and managing accounts
  • logging users in
  • publishing and managing stories and chapters
  • publishing and managing comments
  • accessing member-only features
  • basic technical management and security

4. Technical providers

The public display of the site is provided through GitHub Pages.

Accounts, authentication and storage for the data needed by the site rely on Supabase services.

5. No data resale

Les Comtes de Dark & Cristo does not sell users' personal data.

The site owner does not use user data for advertising purposes.

6. Account and data deletion

When the feature is available, users can delete their own account from their settings.

This deletion is final.

Deleting an account deletes the data and content linked to that account under the conditions provided by the site's operation.

If an account is deleted by administration or moderation, some data may be kept temporarily when strictly necessary to handle a dispute, report, abuse case or security issue.

7. Retention period

Data used by the site is kept for as long as necessary for the site and the user account to function.

Some retention periods may also depend on the technical rules of the services used by the site, including Supabase.

8. User rights

When site tools allow it, users may access, edit or delete their information directly through the features provided.

9. Cookies and technical elements

The site is not intended to use advertising or advertising trackers.

Some technical elements required for login and session maintenance may be used by the services employed by the site.

10. Contact

For any question about this Privacy Policy, contact:

nitroapex@gmail.com
